key_insights.insight: The core truth in life is that the hard thing to do and the right thing to do are usually the same.
key_insights.quote: the core truth I've learned in my life is that the hard thing to do and the right thing to do are usually the same thing

key_insights.insight: Many of our problems accompany us regardless of location, so moving to a new place often doesn't solve anything.
key_insights.quote: all the problems are buying a plane ticket with you Like they're going to Spain with you wherever you go

key_insights.insight: Transitioning in a difficult situation doesn't always require drastic changes; sometimes, small adjustments are sufficient.
key_insights.quote: if it's true that I'm at a place where transitioning it, then just cut back. You don't have to cut all your hair off.

key_insights.insight: Every ending is a precursor to a new beginning, providing an opportunity for growth and change.
key_insights.quote: every beginning is born of some other beginning's end.

key_insights.insight: The goal of a relationship is a great relationship and keep it great. So we're gonna hear it that way.  We're not gonna hear it as a criticism.
key_insights.quote: we're hearing this with love, we're hearing this as a practice, it's a deliberate practice because our goal is to have a great relationship and keep it great. So we're gonna hear it that way. We're not gonna hear it as a criticism

key_insights.insight: It's not enough to find a romantic partner; consciously tend to the relationship to maintain its health.
key_insights.quote: It's like you forgot to water the plant.Like, and nobody meant to kill the plant, but you killed the plant because you weren't thinking about the plant.

key_insights.insight: Relationship 'slippage' occurs through small, unnoticed choices that accumulate over time.
key_insights.quote: Slippage is...just little raindrops that eventually become the flood

key_insights.insight: Maintaining a fulfilling relationship involves paying attention to the individual needs of each partner, as well as the needs of the couple.
key_insights.quote: Pay attention. And pay attention to three things. The you, the me, and the we. Because those are three different things.

actionable_advice.how_to: Rather than engaging in conversations with harsh entry points try doing it via email so that your partner has time to reflect.

actionable_advice.advice: Share what your partner didn't do perfectly that week.
actionable_advice.how_to: Make this a deliberate practice with the goal to keep the relationship great by sharing two or three things that a person did not do perfectly in a relationship that week, deliberately hearing this with love.

actionable_advice.advice: Share what your partner did well that week.
actionable_advice.how_to: Deliberately share three things that the person did that made you feel loved or feel good

actionable_advice.advice: When initiating a discussion about spicing things up in the bedroom, create curiosity and be playful.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of confronting your partner with blame, mention a dream you had about them and reveal the thing you'd be interested in doing.

memorable_stories: An anecdote about a case where James Sexton won a case for an abusive client because the opposing lawyer didn't know how to present a photo as evidence.
Story about a hairdresser who played a trick and made only small changes to their hairstyle for women who wanted to change their life.
A story about the guest's mom and the chicken dance at his wedding and the value of a photo that immortalized this memory.
A personal story about how seeing another couple's affection at a theme park made him realize he didn't love his wife like that.
contrarian_claims: Marriage and love have very little to do with each other and is largely performative.
Marriage is an incredibly bad/dangerous idea with an unbelievably bad failure rate.
Happy wife, happy life is an unbelievably ridiculous model of relationship.
The most important thing in people's lives and their greatest accomplishment should not be their children.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Divorce Expert: Slippage Is Tearing Marriages Apart! If Kids Are Your Top Priority & You Spot This You’ll Divorce In 1-3 Years!
